title: The Sun Has Set
content:
The sun has set, and the long grass now
    Waves dreamily in the evening wind;
    And the wild bird has flown from that old gray stone
    In some warm nook a couch to find.

    In all the lonely landscape round
    I see no light and hear no sound,
    Except the wind that far away
    Come sighing o'er the healthy sea.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Bronte, titled "The Sun Has Set", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
